expectations.text: Recognizes Nx + Angular + .NET + Docker and produces a coherent plan covering all four
expectations.evidence: PLAN.md covers Nx, Angular, .NET, Docker with coherent numbered steps

expectations.text: Dockerfile installs both Node and the .NET SDK in a single image
expectations.evidence: Dockerfile uses FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0 and installs Node 20 via NodeSource
PLAN.md step 3 uses `create-nx-workspace@latest . --preset=apps` — NOT --preset=angular-monorepo. Uses the generic 'apps' preset instead.

expectations.text: Plan includes `nx add @nx/dotnet` for .NET plugin registration
expectations.evidence: PLAN.md step 4 uses `npm install -D @nx/angular @nx-dotnet/core`. Wrong plugin (community @nx-dotnet/core rather than official @nx/dotnet), and uses npm install rather than `nx add`.
PLAN.md contains no mention of configure-ai-agents, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or Nx MCP. AI-agents step entirely absent.

expectations.text: Plan scaffolds a sample Angular app AND a sample .NET project so the workspace is immediately functional
expectations.evidence: Step 6 generates Angular app `web`; step 7 generates .NET api. Both present.

expectations.text: Assistant does not silently skip the AI-agents configuration step
expectations.evidence: The prompt is terse so could arguably not mention AI agents; however the skill-informed version did include it, and the baseline silently dropped it
